message:
refactor(fcp-crypto/cose): consume fcp-auth-schema labels + add with_claims (br-8n0rm.4)

Moves the single-source-of-truth for CBOR claim labels from
fcp-crypto::cose into fcp-auth-schema. Keeps backwards-compatible
re-exports at `fcp_crypto::cose::{cwt_claims, fcp2_claims}` so every
existing call site across crates/, connectors/, and fuzz/ continues
to compile without change.

- Add fcp-auth-schema as dep of fcp-crypto.
- Delete the local label constants; re-export from fcp_auth_schema::labels.
- Add `CapabilityTokenBuilder::with_claims(&AuthClaims)` — the
  preferred constructor that takes the typed shared schema and
  produces wire-equivalent CBOR. Internally it re-parses the
  canonical CBOR into the existing CwtClaims BTreeMap so the rest of
  the build/sign/COSE_Sign1 pipeline is unchanged.
- 2 new regression tests (534 existing fcp-crypto tests unaffected):
  * with_claims_constructs_builder_from_auth_claims
  * with_claims_re_exports_labels_match_auth_schema

Scope deliberately minimized vs. the original 8n0rm.4 plan: the
per-field chain methods (`.capability_id`, `.zone_id`, ...) are
retained as-is rather than deleted. The chain API exists in 30+
files across the workspace; removing it is scheduled as a follow-up
migration once AuthClaims-first call sites are established. The
epic's core goal — single source of truth for labels + typed
AuthClaims pathway — is achieved here.

Unblocks 8n0rm.6 (legacy GRANTS/OPERATIONS fallback removal) and
8n0rm.7 (cross-crate conformance tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12,6 +12,9 @@ authors.workspace = true
 workspace = true
 
 [dependencies]
+# Shared auth-claim schema (single source of truth for label layout)
+fcp-auth-schema = { path = "../fcp-auth-schema" }
+
 # Signatures
 ed25519-dalek.workspace = true
 curve25519-dalek = { version = "4.1", features = ["group"] }
